title: Bitbucket の 503 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article contains outdated information regarding Bitbucket Pipelines plan limits, which is a critical factual claim.
Most other technical claims and troubleshooting steps are accurately supported by current official documentation and community resources.
The article effectively identifies common causes of 503 errors in Bitbucket and provides actionable resolution steps.

sources.title: Retry-After header - HTTP - MDN Web Docs
sources.claim: The HTTP Retry-After response header indicates how long the user agent should wait before making a follow-up request. In a 503 Service Unavailable response, this indicates how long the service is expected to be unavailable.

sources.title: Mastering the Retry-After HTTP Header | by Vipul Malhotra | Apr, 2026 | Medium
sources.claim: The Retry-After HTTP response header indicates how long the client should wait before making a follow-up request. It's a standardized way for servers to communicate backoff periods to clients, preventing unnecessary load and providing a better user experience. It is typically used with status codes like 503 Service Unavailable.

sources.title: Retry-After - Expert Guide to HTTP headers
sources.claim: The Retry-After header appears in responses where the server needs the client to delay before retrying. It is commonly paired with 503 Service Unavailable where the server is temporarily down for maintenance or overloaded.

sources.title: Retry-After Header - Sportmonks
sources.claim: The `Retry-After` HTTP response header tells a client how long to wait before making a follow-up request. It appears on `503 Service Unavailable` responses (the server is temporarily unable to handle requests). The value is either an integer number of seconds or an HTTP-date indicating the exact moment when the server will accept new requests.

sources.title: Retry-After Header - ModHeader
sources.claim: The Retry-After response HTTP header indicates the time that the client must wait before attempting another request to a specific server. It is primarily used in instances where overload or maintenance is underway.

sources.title: Bitbucket Status
sources.claim: Bitbucket provides a status page to check for service incidents, including those related to pipelines and general accessibility issues.

sources.title: Bitbucket Status. Check if Bitbucket is down or having an outage. | StatusGator
sources.claim: StatusGator monitors Bitbucket outages, downtime, and service disruptions. It lists incidents like 'Pipelines unavailable due to 503 errors' and 'Service not loading or accessible'. Bitbucket posts separate notifications for planned maintenance work.

sources.title: Limitations of Bitbucket Pipelines - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Bitbucket Pipelines have limits on concurrent executions and build minutes per plan. As of November 2025, the Free plan includes 50 min/month and up to 10 concurrent steps. The Standard plan includes 2,500 min/month, and the Premium plan includes 3,500 min/month.

sources.title: Evolving Bitbucket Pipelines: Concurrency Groups and Environments for Flexible Deployments | Atlas Bench
sources.claim: Bitbucket Pipelines provides the concurrency-group option to limit the number of concurrent builds. When multiple steps belong to the same concurrency group, Bitbucket will run them sequentially rather than all at once, automatically handling queuing.

sources.title: All remaining pipeline jobs fail after period of time - Atlassian Community
sources.claim: Bitbucket Pipeline jobs can get stuck in a 'Queued' state indefinitely and eventually fail with messages like 'Could not assign the step to a runner as all runners are busy', indicating that the queue may be full or resources are exhausted.

sources.title: How to fix 503 Error code from Webhooks response | Bitbucket Cloud | Atlassian Support
sources.claim: A 503 error can occur if Bitbucket IPs are not allowlisted as inbound traffic at the user's end or if the port on which a webhook is running is not open for inbound traffic, indicating firewall or network restriction issues.

sources.title: Git commands return error code 503 | Bitbucket Data Center - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Git commands returning a 503 error from Bitbucket Server can be caused by a proxy returning the error due to Bitbucket Server being down, or by Bitbucket Server hitting internal resource limits (server overload).

sources.title: Bitbucket - 503 Service Unavailable - post upgrade to 5.14 - Atlassian Community
sources.claim: 503 Service Unavailable errors can be encountered when Bitbucket is running behind a reverse proxy, and troubleshooting often involves checking proxy logs and Bitbucket application logs.

sources.title: HTTP timeouts and errors 502, 503, 504, 499 logged by reverse proxy with Bitbucket Data Center - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: When accessing Bitbucket over HTTP, a reverse proxy can report HTTP errors 502, 503, 504 if there's no reply from Bitbucket or it's too slow, often due to a large number of simultaneous connections.

sources.title: Deployment concurrency control | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Bitbucket Deployments automatically checks if a deployment is in progress before starting a new one to the same environment, pausing later pipelines to prevent concurrent deployments.

sources.title: Fix Git SSL Certificate Issues: A Simple Step-by-Step Guide - Cloudways
sources.claim: Git relies on the system's certificate store to verify SSL certificates. If certificates are outdated, Git may not trust the remote server's certificate, leading to an error. Updating Git is often the simplest solution as newer versions have updated CA bundles.

sources.title: Git SSL Certificate Errors - Fix Clone and Push Failures - FixMyCert
sources.claim: Common causes for Git SSL certificate errors include outdated Git with an old CA bundle, corporate proxies intercepting HTTPS (SSL inspection), and self-signed certificates on internal Git servers. Updating Git is a quick fix, as newer Git versions have updated CA bundles.

sources.title: Fixing the Git fatal: unable to access Error: SSL Certificate Problem (Self-Signed Certificate in… - Luca Berton
sources.claim: When connecting to a Git server over HTTPS, Git (via cURL) validates the server's SSL certificate. If the server uses a self-signed certificate or one not issued by a globally trusted CA, Git will see it as untrustworthy and abort the operation with an SSL certificate problem.

sources.title: Command failed git update-git-for-windows - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: SSL certificate problems with curl can manifest as 'curl: (60) SSL certificate problem: self signed certificate in certificate chain' errors, indicating a failure to verify the server's legitimacy.

sources.title: Override and mock network responses #DevToolsTips - YouTube
sources.claim: Chrome DevTools allows users to open the network tab, inspect requests, and view response headers. It also includes features to override response headers.

sources.title: How to Override Response Headers with Chrome DevTools - tweak
sources.claim: Modern browsers like Chrome provide developer tools (accessed by F12) to inspect HTTP requests, including the response headers, within the Network tab.

sources.title: Why do Chrome's developer tools fail to always collect response data? - Super User
sources.claim: Developer tools (like Chrome's) allow examining network requests and responses by selecting the failed request in the Network tab and viewing its Headers tab.

sources.title: Environment Variables - Git
sources.claim: Git includes a complete set of traces that can be enabled using environment variables like GIT_TRACE=1 (for general traces) and GIT_TRACE_PERFORMANCE=1 (for performance data) to output detailed debugging information to stderr or a specified file.

sources.title: Troubleshooting Git - GitLab Docs
sources.claim: Git includes a complete set of traces for debugging commands, such as GIT_TRACE_PERFORMANCE=1 for tracing performance data and GIT_TRACE=1 for general traces, which can provide detailed output.

sources.title: how to debug git-lfs source code #5691 - GitHub
sources.claim: Using the GIT_TRACE=1 environment variable (e.g., `GIT_TRACE=1 git clone`) can help analyze the sequence of events during a Git command, providing verbose output.

sources.title: Git verbose connection diagnostics - Scientific Computing
sources.claim: The Git trace capability (e.g., `export GIT_TRACE=1` for macOS/Linux) can give more details for diagnosing connection issues regardless of the protocol used.

sources.title: Really verbose way to test Git connection over SSH? - Ask Ubuntu
sources.claim: Environmental variables like GIT_TRACE and GIT_TRACE_PACKET can be set to true (e.g., `GIT_TRACE=true git clone`) to provide useful debugging output for Git commands.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: Update the Bitbucket Pipelines plan limits section to reflect the latest information from Atlassian's official documentation.
Optional improvement: Consider adding dates to factual claims that might change over time, such as plan details or specific error messages, or explicitly state that these might be subject to change.
